disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset over the last ~3 hours of frequent gagging and heavy pooled oral secretions in a 3-day-old baby
trigger_question_examples: What has been happening in the last few hours?
Can you describe the gagging and the secretions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: About three hours ago he suddenly started gagging over and over, and there is a lot of spit and saliva pooling in his mouth. It has been almost constant and nothing we do settles it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feeding intolerance for ~12 hours — every attempted oral feed is regurgitated immediately
trigger_question_examples: Is he keeping any feeds down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: For about the last twelve hours every time we try to feed him the milk comes straight back up. He can't seem to keep anything down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Persistent grimacing, restlessness and apparent discomfort; carers feel unable to comfort him
trigger_question_examples: Does he seem uncomfortable or distressed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He keeps grimacing and seems really unsettled and uncomfortable, and we don't know how to comfort him. It is very hard to watch.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Prescribed comfort medicines (morphine drops) are at home but the carers have not given them out of fear
trigger_question_examples: Has the team given you any medicines for comfort?
Do you have anything prescribed to help him?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The team gave us comfort medicine drops (morphine) to use, but we have been too frightened to give them in case we do something wrong.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever to touch and no witnessed jerking or twitching movements (carer-reported pertinent negatives)
trigger_question_examples: Does he feel hot or have a temperature?
Have you seen any jerking, stiffening or twitching?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He feels a normal temperature when we touch him, and we haven't seen any jerking or twitching — it's more gagging and bringing things up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known congenital condition diagnosed antenatally; baby discharged home 3 days ago for palliative (comfort) care with a community team
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me his background and what the team has told you?
What care is he under at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was born with a serious condition that was found during the pregnancy — the top of his head and part of his brain didn't form. He came home a few days ago for comfort care with a community palliative team.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Both carers are extremely distressed, exhausted and grieving, and describe feeling overwhelmed
trigger_question_examples: How are you managing at home right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly we are exhausted and frightened. Watching him like this is devastating and we feel completely overwhelmed and unsure what to do.
